name: Iraq
summary: Day 60 Monday. WSJ IRAQ-MILITIA ATTRIBUTION ARCHITECTURE PERSISTS — APRIL 12 SAUDI MoFA PROTEST SUMMONS CONFIRMED, QAANI-BAGHDAD VISIT REPORTED, NAMED MILITIAS KATAIB HEZBOLLAH/ASAIB AHL AL-HAQ. WSJ April 21 framing (relayed via ISW) attributing approximately half of the ~1,000 drone attacks on Saudi Arabia during the war to Iran-backed Iraqi militias remains REPORTED single-WSJ-source pending Saudi MoD or CENTCOM independent confirmation. Operational architecture: (1) April 12 Saudi MoFA formal protest summons of Iraq's ambassador CONFIRMED via House of Saud + Defense News — warning Saudi Arabia 'would take necessary measures to ensure its national security and territorial integrity'; (2) IRGC Quds Force commander Esmail Qaani reportedly visited Baghdad during escalation period — REPORTED single-source via WSJ, indicates direct Tehran-Baghdad militia command coordination; (3) Named militias Kataib Hezbollah and Asaib Ahl al-Haq (post-2003 US-invasion origin) targeting Yanbu oil hub, Saudi Eastern Province oil installations, Kuwait airport, Bahrain sites. UNN relays as 'covert war' framing. Iraqi government silence on Saudi protest summons holds Day 60. Hormuz closure persists — Iraqi southern oil exports remain at ~900K bpd vs pre-war 3.3M bpd; April 23 ISDB shows 5 vessels all dark via Northern Corridor (88% suppression). Trump cancellation of Witkoff-Kushner dispatch (Saturday EDT) removes near-term Iraq-restart contingency; Trump 3-5 day patience window expired Sunday April 26 23:59 IST without strike — Iraq-restart sequencing now contingent on either Pakistan-Oman-Russia mediation track yielding or US blockade lifting. [TRUNCATED — needs re-synthesis]

current.restart_potential: 1.5-2.0M bpd over 2-4 weeks if FM lifted

recent_signals.headline: DAY 60 — IRAQ FORCE MAJEURE PERSISTS, NO RESTART SEQUENCING POST TRUMP CANCELLATION

historical_summaries.summary: 2 signals. Iranian drones struck coalition military camps near Erbil and Baghdad, wounding US soldiers | Iraq declared force majeure on all foreign-operated oilfields due to Hormuz disruption

7 signals. Force majeure on all foreign-operated oilfields persists — Day 17 with no resolution | Iraqi-linked oil tanker exited Hormuz via Iran on April 5 per Bloomberg Hormuz Tracker — Iraq is in Iran's three-tier 'friendly' category | IRAQ DIRECT BENEFICIARY OF CEASEFIRE — FORCE MAJEURE PATH TO RESTART
